status: experimental
description: |

author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering

modified: 2026/07/06

    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: network_connection
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        EventID:
            - 4625
            - 4648
            - 4728
        LogonType: 3
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative access
    - Service account activity
level: high
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2018-10933 — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-06T08:33:36Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "CVE-2018-10933", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2018-10933 Exploitation Attempt - GENERIC"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|90 90 90 90|"; rawbytes; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:7409361; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-06, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)

items.title: fast-mcp-telegram: Bearer token path traversal bypasses reserved Telegram session protection
items.description:
## Summary

fast-mcp-telegram validates HTTP Bearer tokens by joining the raw token string into a session-file path. The verifier rejects the exact reserved token `telegram`, but it does not reject path separators or normalize the path before checking whether the session file exists. A remote HTTP client can therefore authenticate as the default legacy session with a token such as `../fast-mcp-telegram/telegram` when the documented default session file `~/.config/fast-mcp-telegram/telegram.session` exists.

This bypasses the reserved session name control that is intended to prevent HTTP multi-user sessions from colliding with the default stdio or legacy account. With account-prefixed MCP tools enabled, the attacker still sees and calls the prefixed tools for the default account, so the prefix middleware does not stop the session selection bypass.

## Impact

An unauthenticated network client can access the Telegram account represented by the default `telegram.session` file without know
items.recommended_sla_action: URGENT PATCH — Critical severity, deploy fix within 72 hours

items.sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-14734 A flaw has been found in SourceCodester Class and Exam Timetablin
id: 80c28510-6576-5d75-a815-58e6768ae9bf
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2026-14734 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: HIGH | Class: RCE
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-14734
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/06
modified: 2026/07/06
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: process_creation
    product: generic
detection:
    selection_process:
        CommandLine|contains:
            - 'cmd.exe /c'
            - 'powershell -enc'
            - '/bin/sh -c'
            - 'wget http'
            - 'curl http'
    selection_webshell:
        Image|endswith:
            - '\cmd.exe'
            - '\powershell.exe'
            - '/bash'
            - '/sh'
        ParentImage|contains:
            - 'w3wp.exe'
            - 'httpd'
            - 'nginx'
            - 'apache2'
            - 'tomcat'
    condition: selection_process or selection_webshell
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative scripts executing via web server
    - Deployment automation tools
level: high
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-14734 Remote Code Execution Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-06T08:33:36Z
// Deploy to: Microsoft Sentinel / Defender XDR
let lookback = 1d;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where (InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe", "httpd.exe", "nginx.exe", "tomcat.exe", "java.exe")
    and FileName in~ ("cmd.exe", "powershell.exe", "bash", "sh", "python.exe", "python3"))
    or (ProcessCommandLine has_any ("wget http", "curl http", "certutil -urlcache", "bitsadmin /transfer"))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| where not(ProcessCommandLine has_any ("update", "install", "backup", "deploy"))  // filter known-good
| extend Severity = "HIGH", CVE = "CVE-2026-14734", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by Timestamp desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2026-14734 Exploitation Attempt - RCE"; flow:established,to_server; content:"/bin/sh"; nocase; content:"cmd.exe"; nocase; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:8699747; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-06, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)
items.recommended_sla_action: SCHEDULED PATCH — Apply in next maintenance window

items.title: golang.org/x/image/tiff has excessive resource consumption in PackBits decompression
items.description: The TIFF decoder does not place a limit on the size of PackBits-compressed data. A maliciously-crafted image can exploit this to cause a small image (both in terms of pixel width/height and encoded size) to make the decoder decode large amounts of compressed data.
items.recommended_sla_action: SCHEDULED PATCH — Apply in next maintenance window

items.title: Keycloak: Unauthorized access via improper validation of encrypted SAML assertions
items.description: Keycloak's SAML broker endpoint does not properly validate encrypted assertions when the overall SAML response is not signed. An attacker with a valid signed SAML assertion can exploit this by crafting a malicious SAML response, injecting an encrypted assertion for an arbitrary principal, leading to unauthorized access and potential information disclosure.
items.confidence_rationale: Single source with minimal corroborating evidence
items.recommended_sla_action: SCHEDULED PATCH — Apply in next maintenance window

items.title: CVE-2026-14731: A weakness has been identified in itsourcecode Hospital Management System 1.0. This affects an unknown part of the file
items.description: A weakness has been identified in itsourcecode Hospital Management System 1.0. This affects an unknown part of the file /patientreport.php. Executing a manipulation of the argument editid can lead to sql injection. The attack can be launched remotely. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks.
items.apex_ai_summary: [MEDIUM] CVE advisory. Risk score 6.3/10. Full APEX AI analysis available to Pro subscribers.

apex.ai_summary: [P2] CVE / Vulnerability - moderate severity (Risk 6.3/10). MITRE ATT&CK mapped: T1190 (1 technique). 1 indicator detected - upgrade to Pro for full IOC access. AI confidence: LOW (25%) - limited source data available.
apex.recommended_action: HIGH PRIORITY - Patch within 72 hours. Monitor affected assets. Review threat hunting queries for related TTPs.

ioc_paywall.message: 1 IOC(s) at 7.0% confidence - unlock with Pro tier.

items.nvd_disclosure: This CVE ID was not found in the NIST NVD database at time of enrichment. Intelligence is preliminary and unverified by NVD. CVSS score and severity are analyst-estimated until NVD confirmation.
items.sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-14731 A weakness has been identified in itsourcecode Hospital Managemen
id: 3e6c6662-a578-5cb1-add2-be1cae1e505d
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2026-14731 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: MEDIUM | Class: RCE
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-14731
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/06
modified: 2026/07/06
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: process_creation
    product: generic
detection:
    selection_process:
        CommandLine|contains:
            - 'cmd.exe /c'
            - 'powershell -enc'
            - '/bin/sh -c'
            - 'wget http'
            - 'curl http'
    selection_webshell:
        Image|endswith:
            - '\cmd.exe'
            - '\powershell.exe'
            - '/bash'
            - '/sh'
        ParentImage|contains:
            - 'w3wp.exe'
            - 'httpd'
            - 'nginx'
            - 'apache2'
            - 'tomcat'
    condition: selection_process or selection_webshell
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative scripts executing via web server
    - Deployment automation tools
level: medium
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-14731 Remote Code Execution Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-06T08:33:36Z
// Deploy to: Microsoft Sentinel / Defender XDR
let lookback = 1d;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where (InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe", "httpd.exe", "nginx.exe", "tomcat.exe", "java.exe")
    and FileName in~ ("cmd.exe", "powershell.exe", "bash", "sh", "python.exe", "python3"))
    or (ProcessCommandLine has_any ("wget http", "curl http", "certutil -urlcache", "bitsadmin /transfer"))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| where not(ProcessCommandLine has_any ("update", "install", "backup", "deploy"))  // filter known-good
| extend Severity = "HIGH", CVE = "CVE-2026-14731", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by Timestamp desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2026-14731 Exploitation Attempt - RCE"; flow:established,to_server; content:"/bin/sh"; nocase; content:"cmd.exe"; nocase; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:2650556; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-06, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)
items.confidence_rationale: Medium confidence — Source 'nvd_cve' is authoritative (government/standards body)
items.recommended_sla_action: SCHEDULED PATCH — Apply in next maintenance window
